name: Yext vs BrightLocal vs Synup
section_which_is_best_for_you:
Yext vs BrightLocal vs Synup: Which is best for you?

Choosing between Yext and BrightLocal for your local listing management needs can be challenging. Both offer robust features, but their approaches and strengths differ. If you're torn between these two popular options, you're not alone. 

This comparison will help you understand the key differences between Yext and BrightLocal, focusing on their core features, pricing, and overall value. We'll also introduce Synup as a third alternative that might also suit your current needs. 

By examining these tools side-by-side, we want to provide you with the information you need to make an informed decision. Whether you're an agency or business managing multiple locations, this guide will help you confidently choose the right local marketing tool for your specific requirements.

Yext vs BrightLocal vs Synup at a Glance

Yext is one of the oldest platforms for managing online listings across a wide network of directories. It offers real-time updates and includes features for review management and local SEO.

BrightLocal focuses on local SEO and citation building. It provides a mix of automated tools and manual services, with strong reporting capabilities. Popular among smaller businesses and agencies.

Synup combines listing management with location-based marketing tools. It’s a first choice for agencies and resellers. It offers a balance between comprehensive listing management and local SEO features such as reputation and local tracking, suitable for businesses of various sizes.

Each tool aims to help businesses manage their online presence, but they differ in their approach, features, and target users. Let's explore these differences to help you decide which one best fits your needs.

‍

‍
section_listing_management:
Yext vs BrightLocal vs Synup: Listing Management

Synup’s listing management tool is API-based and lets you auto-sync your listings across 60+ directories. You can also identify and remove duplicate listings to keep your local SEO health in shape. Ongoing bulk changes to your listings are super easy, you or your clients can log on to the platform and update info for any number of locations in a few clicks.

Yext has a more extensive network of publishers (100+) and is good at handling listing management at scale. They also allow you to make bulk changes to your listings. However, customers report losing access to their listings or not having full ownership of their listings once they leave Yext. It also does a poor job at suppressing duplicate listings.

Brightlocal offers manual submission services for listings and auto-sync for Google, Facebook, Bing, and Apple. You can track what citations your business isn’t present on. But the plan doesn’t include editing the listings created, which is an additional service you have to opt for and pay for. Customers have reported issues with listing accuracy at times.

Yext vs BrightLocal vs Synup: Reputation Management

‍

Synup has an AI-powered reputation management tool that’s built into our listing management offering itself, unlike Yext and Brightlocal. One USP of Synup’s reputation software is that it integrates with CRM and POS software to fetch customer data directly and get reviews from new customers. Synup also helps you automate the review management process and showcase new reviews on your website with beautiful widgets.

Yext’s reputation management tool is still pretty new and needs further improvement. Many basic features that you expect with a ORM solution are lacking – such as AI-automation and review generation. It does a good job at monitoring review if you’re using it as a supplementary (but rather expensive) feature to their listing management offering.

Unlike Yext, Brightlocal has a more well-rounded review management offering. They help you monitor reviews across 80+ sites, generate more reviews via email or SMS, and respond to reviews. However, the tool lacks AI support for review responses and doesn’t integrate with CRM or POS software like Synup does, hence the ratings.

Yext vs BrightLocal vs Synup: Local Analytics

When it comes to local analytics – Synup offers you almost all data points you need to gauge the performance of your business or client. From listing analytics (profile views, phone call, website visits, and more) to local rank tracking (keyword and grid rank tracking solutions), Synup covers everything. The achilles’ heel as we discussed above is Synup’s reporting and visualization, which can be improved further.

Local analytics isn’t Yext’s strong suit. They have some great SEO and listing analytics capabilities for enterprise. But doesn’t offer any location-level or rank tracking capabilities which are important from a local perspective.

Brightlocal has hands-down some of the best analytics solutions in the industry. While their analytics capability is the same as Synup’s, what sets them apart is the focus on reporting and visualization which makes it easy to gather insights from the data.

Yext vs BrightLocal vs Synup: Local SEO

Now this is from an overall local SEO perspective.

Synup has many tools and features that aid the performance of local businesses online. This includes local landing pages and store locators, voice search optimizer, AI-powered local content and posting tools, and even a social media platform. One unique selling point is Synup’s hyper-personalized local campaigns. These campaigns let you run promotions and offers based time and event-triggers such as weather, festivities, etc.

Yext has an extensive suite of features for large businesses and entreprises for local SEO. This includes local pages, an AI-powered chatbot, social media, and search box. They’ve also built some vertical specific marketing solutions for eCommerce such as site search, product searches and reviews, and more.

Yext vs BrightLocal vs Synup: Reseller Support

Synup wins in this category simply because of the stellar support the tool offers to agencies and resellers using the platform. While Yext and Brightlocal allow basic reselling solutions, Synup goes beyond and has a full-fledged platform that not only helps agencies resell Synup but also manage clients, find new leads, prospect better, and operate their agency smoothly. It’s sort of like an agency operations software. Synup also has a whitelabel business scan tool you can integrate with your site to capture leads.

Yext offers integration and partnership program for agencies and consultants as well as other SaaS resellers/platforms. However, the support you get once you partner with them is minimal and given their focus on enterprise segment, they don’t invest as much into growing their partner’s business.

Brightlocal is more agency-oriented than Yext. They offer some good prospecting tools such as business audits for client locations, scan tool for capturing leads, and stellar reporting to help you communicate the impact of your efforts. They also let you whitelabel their platform and reporting. However, there’s little to no support for actually helping you sell or manage your day-to-day client operations.

‍

‍
section_which_should_you_choose:
Yext vs BrightLocal vs Synup: Which Should You Choose?

Depends on which segment you belong to.

For enterprise customers, either Yext or Synup make for a great choice. Yext has somewhat of an upper hand with their primarily enterprise-focused platform and scale of operations. But Synup competes strongly with a more comprehensive featureset that won’t have you look elsewhere.

For SMB businesses, Brightlocal and Synup are promising. Brightlocal’s pricing and certain pay-as-you-go plans for listings are appealing for small businesses. Synup is a good shot if you want to keep your listings updated without the hefty cost build-up.

For agencies and resellers, Synup hands down wins the race. Going beyond just basic whitelabeling and reporting, Synup is building a platform that’s partner-first and helps you grow your business with our tools.

To sum up

I hope you have a more well-shaped opinion on how each of these tools work. We tried to be as transparent in our insights as possible. All three tools have some unique strengths and shortcomings that we’ve covered. After filtering based on the segment you’re in, it’s best to go ahead and try a software for your choice. 

‍
faq_rich_text:
Yext vs BrightLocal vs Synup: Frequently Asked Questions

What is better than Yext?

There are many software that do better than Yext at multiple parameters and features. You can check out Synup as an alternative if you want a similar API-based solution for local marketing with better support. Brightlocal is also a good alternative to Yext in terms of customer support and pricing.

What does BrightLocal do?

Brightlocal is a local marketing software build for SMB businesses and agencies that deal with multiple locations. It offers solutions such as listing management, review management, and reporting capabilities.

What does Yext do?

Yext started as a listing management software, which now deals in enterprise-grade local marketing solutions. It has a range of capabilities – from listing management to eCommerce specific solutions for search.
